Were you to marry now a child would at your breast soon lie, You're not a child now, sister, that the least thing makes you cry. 2. To whine; murmur. 3. To complain.

def: n. f. S. स्था to stop. Cold; chilliness.

s. m. (dakh.) Cold season, cold weather, winter.

adj. (f. -ī), Cold, cool, chill, bleak; frozen; iced, cooled; refreshed; cold-hearted, insensible; dead; pacified, appeased, soothed, allayed, extinguished, set at rest; still, quiet, calm, tranquil; mild, placid, gentle; impotent, lacking virility; dull, inactive (as a market); free from weeds (as a soil)
more: adj. Cold. ٿهنڐا پڙنا ṭhanḍā paṛnā, To abate (anger, virility, or wantonness). ٿهنڐا کرنا ṭhanḍā karnā, v. a. 1. To make cold, cool. 2. To extinguish. 3. To comfort, to assuage, to pacify, appease. ٿهنڐا هونا ṭhanḍā honā, 1. To become cold, to cool. 2. To be extinguished. 3. To be comforted or pacified. 4. To rest. 5. To lose virility. کليجا ٿهنڐا هونا kalejā ṭhanḍā honā, 1. To be pleased, to be happy (by seeing a friend, son, &c.). 2. To have oneʼs revenge gratified.
subs: v.n. To become cold or cool; to abate or become extinguished (as anger, wantonness, virility, &c.)
adv. While it is cool, in the cool of the day, in the morning or evening; — at peace, peaceably
v.n. To heave a deep (lit. cold) sigh, to sigh in despair
v.t. To make cold, to cool; to ice, to freeze; to extinguish (a light, &c.), to quench, allay; to refresh; to soothe, comfort, assuage; to pacify, appease
s.f. Sweetmeat (ḥalwā) made at the close of a feast (when the pan is becoming cool)
s.f. Lukewarmness; a show of love, pretended affection
s.f. A stunted growth; an impotent person

v.n. To become cool or cold, to cool; to rest (after fatigue); to be refreshed; to be extinguished (a light); to be allayed, &c.; to be appeased or pacified; to be comforted; to be subdued or tamed, to submit, yield; to lose virility
(-kā), To have one's longing, or revenge, &c. gratified; to be avenged; to be satisfied; to be solaced or comforted; to be pleased or happy.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀣𑀟𑁆𑀠 (thaḍḍha), 𑀣𑀤𑁆𑀥 (thaddha, “fixed, firm”), from Sanskrit स्तब्ध (stabdha).

def: adj. 1. Cool; cold; chill; rigid; bleak; raw. ' Chhurī mārūṅ ki khanḍā?' 'Khanḍā.' 'Terī mā kā peṭ ṭhanḍā! ' Boys' rhyme. 'Shall I stab you with a knife or a sword?' 'A sword!' 'Bravo! may your mother bring forth! men!' 2. Iced; icy; frozen; frosty; wintry. Phr.—Cold as marble; cold as a frog. 3. Dead; cold as death. 4. Cold-blooded; cold-hearted; insensible. 5. Impotent; weak in the loins or back. 6. Calm; tranquil; still; quiet; serene. 7. Pacified; appeased; composed; soothed. Ho gayā khanjar-i-be-dād kā bismil ṭhanḍā, Le, huā ab to kalejā terā qātil, ṭhanḍā! Rind. The dagger of the tyrant plunged, her victim's breast lies cold, Say, ṁurderess, is your burning hatred now appeased, consoled? 8. Mild; placid; gentle. 9. Inactive; dull (market) ( mandā ). ṭhanḍā k . v. a. 1. To cool; freeze; ice. 2. To extinguish; put out (a light); quench; blow out. 3. To comfort; assuage; pacify; appease. ṭhanḍī garmiyāṅ , Poet. n. f. Lukewarmness; a show of love; pretended affection. ṭhanḍā honā , v. n. 1. See ṭhanḍā , adj. Jab lag kāraj hoë bhūl kāhū se mat kahīye, Dushman tattā hoë, āp ṭhanḍī ho rahīye! Girdhar Kunḍlī. Until your business done do you your tongue restrain, Your foe may foam and rage, do you but cool remain. 2. To take rest; to rest after fatigue. 3. To be tamed; to submit; yield; turn tail. Khūb bhaṛkāyā thā us ko saut ne, Maiṅ huī jab garm, ṭhanḍā ho gayā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. My co-wife did my spouse 'gainst me inflame, When I grew angry too he grew quite tame. kalejā ṭhanḍā honā , v. n. 1. To gratify one's revenge; to be avenged. 2. To be pleased, happy, satisfied, solaced, comforted. ṭhanḍe ṭhanḍe , adv. 1. In the cool of the morning or evening; at dawn or dusk. 2. ( ṭhanḍe peṭoṅ ) At peace; peaceably. Maiṅ khud jalī bhunī hūṅ, mujh se na hūje garm, Bas ṭhanḍe ṭhanḍe, sāhab, tum jāo apne ghar . Wom. Jān Sāhab. ṭhanḍe sāṅs bharnā , v. n. To sigh; heave a deep sigh. ṭhanḍī kaṛāhī , n. f. Sweetmeat ( halwā ) prepared at the close of a feast. Hin. ṭhanḍī maṭṭī , n. f. 1. A stunted growth. 2. An impotent person.
adj. (dakh.) See ٿهنڐا ṭhanḍā.
s.f. Refrigerant medicine; a cooling drink; the intoxicating potion made of bhang.
n. f. 1. A cooling medicine or drink. Garmī kī ṭhanḍāī hai, Meraṭh kā kaserū! Street cry. 2. A draught of bhang .
s. f. 1. Refrigerant medicine. 2. The intoxicating drug made of bhang.
s.f. Coldness, cold, coolness; reduced temperature; a refreshing sensation; pacification; calmness; solace, comfort
subs: (-ke or -ko), To be cooled; to be refreshed; to have a longing, or a desire for revenge, &c. gratified; to be appeased (as rage, enmity, &c.); to be avenged.

def: n. f. 1. Coolness; reduced temperature. 2. Pacification; solace; comfort. 1. Ānkhoṅ sukh, kaleje ṭhanḍak . Prov. The delight of the eye, the joy of the heart. 2. Aṅkhoṅ kā nūr, dil kī ṭhanḍak . Prov. The light of my eye, my heart's delight! ṭhanḍak paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be cooled or refreshed. 2. To be appeased (rage, enmity); to be avenged. Ab to saukan ke paṛī ṭhanḍak, buā, Ghar luṭā, khāvind chhuṭā, bachchā muā! Wom. Parī Khānam. My co-wife, sister, now should be o'erjoyed, Gone home, gone husband, and my child too dead!
s. (dakh.) See ٿهنڐک ṭhanḍak.

Contractor; lease-holder ( ijārah-dār 1); one who farms a licence for the sale of spirituous liquors or the like. ṭheke-dār maurūsī , n. m. An hereditary lessee. ṭheke-dār maurūsī kā istehqāq pesh k . To set up a title as an hereditary lessee. ṭhekā-i-dāimī , n. m. A lease in perpetuity. ṭhekā denā , v. a. 1. See ijārah denā . 2. To give out in piece-work or by the job. ṭhekā dene-vālā , Lessor; grantor. ṭheke kī miyād . The term for which the lease has to run. ṭhekā lenā , v. a. 1. To take or enter into a contract; to contract; lease; farm; to take out or purchase a licence. 2. To monopolize; engross.
s.f. Prop, &c. ( = ṭhek; ṭek; ṭekan).
n. f. 1. A large sack of grain. 2. A resting place ( ṭhek , 2). 3. E. A cork ( ḍāṭ ). ṭhekī lagānā, yā denā , v. a. To rest against (a porter carrying a load).

s.f. The act of resting on the way (by a porter carrying a load); a resting place (as for a porter carrying a load); prop, support ( = ṭhek); a large sack of grain, a load; a plug, cork ( = ṭhepī; ṭheṅṭhī)
more: s. f. Act of resting on the way (by a porter carrying a load). ٿهيکي لگانا يا دينا ṭhekī-lagānā or -denā, To rest (a porter carrying a load).
subs: v.t. To rest (against, as one carrying a load).

def: v. n. 1. To come in collision; knock against. 2. To ground; stick; strike the bottom. 3. ( Rus .) To make meal cakes; to bake. 4. ( Rus .) To fly; run away ( bhāgnā ). Ū ṭhek gayo, mhāre hāth nā āyo . Rus.
v.n. To strike or knock (against), come into collision (with); to touch the bottom, to ground, to stick (as a boat).

n. m. 1. A push; 2. A wheel-barrow; trolly ( ṭhelā-gāṛī ). ṭhelā ṭhelī , n. f. Shoving and jostling.
s.m. Push, shove, thrust, impulse; — ṭhelā, A truck; a trolly; a wheel-barrow
subs: s.m. A share or portion of land acquired by force
ṭhelam-ṭhel, s.f. Shoving and shouldering, pushing, jostling

ety: ṭhel˚ = S. स्थालय(ति), caus. of rt. स्थल्
def: v.t. To push, shove, shoulder, jostle; to move forward by pushing, to propel.
more: v. a. To shove, to push, to move forward by pushing.

def: s.m. Acting the buffoon.
v. a. 1. To push; propel; thrust. 2. To elbow; shoulder; jostle.
n. f. Foolish or vacant laughter; giggle.
s.m. A bludgeon, a small club, a cudgel, a stick; — the thumb ( = ṭhosā); the penis
more: s. m. 1. A stick, a small club. 2. A tool (penis).
subs: v.n. Cudgel to sound, stick to be used; to have a row, to fall out, to use clubs; — to be called a penis, to be called an opprobrious name, to be disgraced, to be jeered or hooted.

def: Wom. n. m. 1. See ṭhosā . 1. Jāne merā ṭheṅgā . Ask my thumb. 2. Sab ko ṭheṅgā dikhātī hai . Wom. She shows her thumb to every body. (She cares for nobody). 2. A club; a cudgel. Zabardast kā ṭheṅgā sir par . Prov. All feel the strong man's thumb. 3. Penis. Maiṅ unko ṭhiṅge pe mārtā hūṅ . Slang. ṭheṅgā bājnā , v. n. To be hooted; hissed; laughted at; jeered. Jiskā kām usī ko sāje, aur kare to ṭheṅgā bāje . Prov. Each person's work doth him become, another's only shown the thumb. (His failure is greeted with hisses).
v. a. To spoil.
s.m. A cudgeller.

ety: ṭheṅg(ā)+nā = lā = Prk. अडओ = S. ल+कः
def: adj. Stumpy, short, &c. = ṭhingnā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Of small stature, short, dwarfish, diminutive (syn. nāṭā).
adj. (dakh.) Short in stature, dwarfish; dwarf ( Harris ).
(or ṭhaiṅs, Harris ) s. (dakh.) Stumble: see ٿهيس ṭhes. ٿهينس کهانا ṭheṅs khānā, To trip against a stone, &c., to make a false step.
v.t. = ṭhesnā, q.v. v.t. To thrust, to pierce; to knock, push, shove; to strike against; to thrust or ram in, to cram, stuff; to eat voraciously ( = ṭhosnā).
adj. = ṭheṭh, q.v. adj. Pure, genuine; real, true, actual, very; good, sound; chaste, idiomatic (as language); — contentious.
adj. 1. Pure, genuine. 2. Contentious.
s.f. Silly and noisy laughter, giggle ( = ṭhī-ṭhī); cf. ṭeṅ-ṭeṅ.
s.f. A plug, cork, bung ( = ṭhepī; ṭhekī); a lurap or ball of ear-wax; a dhotī or cloth reaching as far as the knee.
more: s. f. 1. A cork, a plug. 2. A ball of ear-wax. 3. A dhotī or cloth reaching no further than the knee.

def: n. f. A ball of ear-wax. Kānoṅ kī ṭheṅṭiyāṅ nikalvāo .
s.f. = ṭheṅṭhī, q.v. s.f. A plug, cork, bung ( = ṭhepī; ṭhekī); a lurap or ball of ear-wax; a dhotī or cloth reaching as far as the knee.
s.m. Bed (of a garden); border.
s.m. = ṭheʼonā, q.v.
more: s. m. The knee; the pastern (of a horse).

def: s.f. Stopple, stopper, plug, cork, bung
more: s. f. A cork, a plug, a stopple. ٿهيپ منـﮩـ مين دينا ṭhepī muṅh meṅ denā, To be silent.
subs: adj. Stopped with a plug, having a stopper or plug or cork, corked
(kisī-ke), To stop the mouth (of anyone), to silence, to gag; to stop (one's own, -apne) mouth, to be silent.

def: Tir. n. f. A cork; plug; stopple; stopper. ṭhepī muṅh meṅ denā , v. a. To close one's mouth; to gag; silence. ṭhepī-dār , adj. Corked; stopped with a plug, etc.
n. f. 1. A knock; blow. 2. See ṭhokar , 1. ṭhes lagnā , v. n. To be knocked against; to trip against a stone, etc. Ṭhes lāge, budh bāṛhe . Prov. Get knocks, get sense.

s.f. Knock, blow; thrust, push, shove; striking the foot (against an obstacle), tripping (against); — ṭhes lagnā, v.n. To be knocked (against), to receive a knock, &c.; to trip (against = ṭhokar lagnā).
more: s. f. A knock, a blow, tripping against a stone, &c. a shove, a push, a thrust.

def: v. a. To knock against.
v.t. To thrust, to pierce; to knock, push, shove; to strike against; to thrust or ram in, to cram, stuff; to eat voraciously ( = ṭhosnā).
more: v. a. 1. To pierce. 2. To knock against. 3. To stuff, to cram, to ram in. 4. To eat ravenously.

def: s.m. Jeer, taunt, sneer, indirect reflection, innuendo.
more: s. m. Sneer, oblique reflection or innuendo.

def: Bhoj. n. m. A boy ( laṛkā ); son.
adj. Pure, genuine; real, true, actual, very; good, sound; chaste, idiomatic (as language); — contentious.
adj. H. ṭhīk right. 1. Sterling; standard; good. 2. Pure; genuine; unalloyed; idiomatic. Ṭheṭh Hindī . 3. Real; true. Ṭheṭh gaṅvār .
s.m. Setting (of a stone); stone set in a ring.
more: s. m. A stone set in a ring.

def: s.m. The place for a stone in a ring ( = thewā).
n. m. H. ṭhā place. The place for a stone in a ring.
s.m. The knee; the pastern (of a horse).
n. f. Sounds of laughter.
s.m. = H تهگلي थिगली thiglī, s.f. = theglī, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) A patch: see پيوند paiwand.

def: s.f. = H تهگلا थिगला thiglā, s.m. = theglī, q.v.

s.m. A mud pillar, a boundary mark ( = ṭhiyā, q.v.). s.m. A mound, heap, or lump (of earth); a block or mass (of stone, wood, &c.); a mud pillar, a boundary mark (cf. thūʼā; ṭhīhā; ḍhūʼā; ḍhiyā); a support; a place to sit on, a seat.
s.f. The knee ( = ṭheʼonā, q.v.).
(caus. of تهجنا) v. a. (dakh.) To congeal ( Harris ).
v. n. (dakh.) To be astonished.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To be congealed ( Harris ).

def: adj. Firm, strong, compact; stable; ascertained, established, well-founded, certain; precise, exact, true, just, correct, accurate, right; even, complete; fit, meet, proper; reasonable; appropriate, suitable, apposite; corresponding, answering; regular, according to rule; in due form; well-formed, well-proportioned; straight, in order, well-conducted; — adv. Exactly, precisely, just; accurately, correctly; duly, justly, agreeably to; truly, in truth; to the point or purpose; completely; clearly, &c.; just so!, precisely!, very true!, all very fine!; — s.m. Precise total, sum, addition; — fixed or permanent residence; whereabouts ( = ṭhikānā); certainty, certain knowledge, &c. (in all these senses it seems questionable whether ṭhīk, although it may be conveniently rendered in English as a subst. = ṭhikānā, is not properly an adj.: it is invariably constructed with a genitive; e.g. uskā kyā ṭhīk hai, 'What is there certain in respect of him?' or 'What certainty is there of him?'; ṭumhārā bhī kahīṅ ṭhīk hai, 'Have you a fixed abode anywhere?')
more: 1. adj. Exact, even, accurate, complete, just, fit, meet, proper, reasonable, true, regular, right; adv. Exactly, &c. 2. ( met. ) Parsimonious. 3. s. m. Sum, addition. ٿهيک ٿهاک ṭhīk ṭhāk, adj. Exact, fit, proper, accurate; properly, fitly, accurately, adequately. ٿهيک آنا ṭhīk ānā, v. n. To fit, correspond, suit, answer, apply. ٿهيک ٿهاک کرنا ṭhīk ṭhāk karnā, v. a. To put to rights, correct, adjust, fit. ٿهيک کرنا ṭhīk karnā, v. a. 1. To put to rights, to correct, to adjust, to adapt, fit. 2. To beat.
subs: v.n. To prove true, turn out right or correct; to come right; to be right, just, or adequate; to succeed; to answer, suit, fit, correspond, apply; — ṭhīk banānā (-ko), To make right or exact, &c.; to put (one) to rights, to bring (one) to his senses; to beat ( = ṭhīk karnā)

adj. & adv. Exact, accurate; fit, proper; — quite right, right and tight, all right; properly, fitly, accurately, adequately; in due order, duly adjusted, put to rights, put straight, &c.; — s.m. Exactitude, &c.; — ṭhīk-ṭhāk karnā (-ko), To put to rights, to correct, adjust, fit, &c. ( = ṭhīk karnā)
adj. (emphat. of ṭhīk), The exact, very exact, &c. (see ṭhīk); approximate; tolerable, passable
ṭhīkam-ṭhīk, ṭhīkoṅ-ṭhīk, adv. Exactly, precisely, just, &c. = ṭhīk, q.v.

(-ko), To render exact, right, true, or fit, &c.; to correct, amend, rectify, put to rights, adjust, adapt; to settle, set in order, arrange; to complete, make up; to regulate; to ascertain, establish, verify; to make certain; to determine; to put (one) to rights, bring (one) to his senses, to beat, chastise
ex: اَپْنی غَلْطِیاں جَھٹ سے ٹِھیک کَرْنا چَاہِئے۔
apnī ġaltiyā̃ jhaṭ se ṭhīk karnā cāhie.
You should quickly correct your mistakes.

def: To ascertain.

adv. 1. All square; all right. 2. Repaired; done up; set up. ṭhīk miltī . Precisely applicable. ṭhīk jānnā, yā mānnā , v. a. To receive; allow; admit the accuracy of; approve. ṭhīk samajh . Sound mind; sound judgment; compos mentis; mens sana . ṭhīk samaë, yā waqt , Juncture; point of time; the nick of time; crisis. ṭhīk karnā , v. a. 1. To render true; to fit; adapt; adjust; square; reconcile. 2. To set; regulate; settle; arrange. 3. To rectify; put to rights; correct; alter. Meṅhdī maiṅ jab-tak lagāūṅ tab tak, aë manhyārī, tū Ṭhīk-kar joṛā, qarīne se lagā, rī chūṛyāṅ . Rangīn. While I put on the henna, bangle-maker, would just you Arrange the set of bangles in their place and fix them true. 4. To cure; remedy; reform; mend; amend. 5. To complete; finish; perfect; make up. 6. To make certain; ascertain; determine, get, or arrive, at the truth. 7. To make good; establish; substantiate; confirm; verify; prove. ṭhīk banānā, yā karnā v. a. To chastise; box one's ears; lick into shape; give a dressing; bring to one's senses or bearings. ghaṛī kī chāl ṭhīk karnā , v. a. To regulate a time-piece. ṭhīk na kahnā , v. n. To give crooked answers; to shuffle; fence; prevaricate; equivocate; to play fast and loose.

s.m. Contract; work done by contract or by the job, piece-work; a task, job; hire, fare; license; lease, farm; mortgage; rest (after fatigue); accompanying a singer (on the ḍhol or other musical instrument); andante (in music)
more: s. m. 1. Hire, fare, fixed price. 2. Work done by contract, a task.
subs: (-kā), To play an accompaniment, to accompany a singer (on the ḍhol, &c.)
s.f. A lease, a farm; a farm held on lease
s.m. A deed of lease, a document conveying a lease or farm
s.m. A lease or farm of which the rent is paid in advance
s.m. Contractor; lease-holder, farmer, lessee; one who farms a license (for the sale of spirituous liquors or the like)
(-ko), To give a contract (to), to lease, to let in farm; — ṭhekā dene-wālā, s.m. Lessor, grantor
s.f. The period for which a contract, &c., is granted; the term for which a lease has to run
(-kā), To take on contract, to contract (for); to lease, farm; to take out a license; to monopolize, engross
s.m. A lease for the term of one's life.

def: adv. Exactly, truly, rightly.
s. m. Abuse.
s. m. (dakh.) See ٿهکانا ṭhikānā.

ety: S. स्थिर+कृत+स्थान+कः; or ṭika, rt. of ṭiknā+S. स्थान+कः
def: s.m. Fixed or permanent place or abode; proper place; precise or exact spot, whereabouts; place, spot, site; dwelling.place, abode, residence, home, quarters; special field or scene of a labour, &c.), beat (as of a barber, or sweeper, &c.); fixing, fixity, certainty; certain knowledge; consistency, harmony, appositeness (of speech, conduct, &c.); origin, source; ground, basis, foundation (of a thing); goal, destination, end, limit, boundary
more: s. m. 1. Residence, place, station, site, abode, dwelling. 2. Boundary, limit, fixing. ٿهکانا ڐهونڐهنا ṭhikānā ḍhūndhnā, To seek a residence or employment. ٿهکانا کرنا ṭhikānā karnā, To account for, to search or trace an affair to its commencement. ٿهکانا لگانا ṭhikānā lagānā, 1. To establish, to settle, to prove. 2. To find out oneʼs residence. ٿهکاني لگانا ṭhikāne lagānā, 1. To put to death, to kill, to waylay, to make away with, despatch, to assassinate. 2. To establish, settle, arrange. ٿهکاني لگنا ṭhikāne lagnā, To be killed, to die, to be put an end to, to be terminated.
v. a. (dakh.) To cheat, to deceive, to impose on, defraud, beguile, bamboozle, delude, gull: see ٿهگنا ṭhagnā.
subs: To distribute money among servants (on the decease of a member of a family)
(-ke wāst̤e, &c.), To seek a residence or employment (for)
v.n. To remain stationary, fixed, or stable; to abide in one course, &c.
adj. (f. -ī), Congruous, consistent, harmonious, apposite, to the point; sensible

(-kā), To put in (its) proper place; to find a place (for), to make room (for); to arrange; to take note (of), to learn (about), to account (for), to search or trace (an affair) to its source or commencement; to find a husband or wife (for)
(-kā), To find out, discover, trace (one's residence, &c.); to trace to the source, to establish, settle, prove
(-ko), To dispose of; to establish, settle, arrange; to make away with, to spend, consume, run through; to send to (his or her) long home, to despatch, put to death, kill, assassinate
v.n. To be still, be at rest, have a home, &c.; to be disposed of, be despatched, be put to death; to be put an end to, be terminated

adj. & adv. Out of place, misplaced; irregular; incoherent, disconnected; inconsistent; indeterminate, unsettled, indefinite; immoderate, disproportioned; uncertain; groundless, unreasonable; — irregularly, incoherently, &c.; indiscriminately, at random.
wety: Related to ٹھیک (ṭhīk). Cognate with Punjabi ਟਿਕਾਣਾ (ṭikāṇā) / ٹِکانا (ṭikānā), Gujarati ઠેકાણું (ṭhekāṇũ), Marathi ठिकाण (ṭhikāṇ), Bengali ঠিকানা (ṭhikana).

def: n. m. S. स्था to stand. 1. Place; site. See استهان (1, 2.) 2. Abode; residence. Tumhārā bhī kahīṅ ṭhikānā hai? 3. A community, or a collection of houses in which a barber, sweeper, etc. have a hereditary monopoly to pursue their profession. Ek ke ṭhikāne meṅ dūsrā halālkhor nahīṅ kamā saktā . 4. A direction or address ( patā , 2.). Sakhī vāhī des mohe jānā, Jā ko nāṅoṅ hai na ṭhikānā . Bhajan. Hymn. Friend, I for that strange land am bound, Whose name or sign-post none hath found. 5. Relations; relative; friends. 6. Centre; nucleus; point of attraction. Jahāṅ khānā, vahāṅ sab kā ṭhikānā . Prov. Where the dish goes round all wanderers (or addresses wanted) will be found. 7. End; limit. 8. Reliance; faith; trust; dependance. 1. Terī bāt kā kuchh ṭhikānā nahīṅ? 2. Divānah hai, valekin bāt kahtā hai ṭhikāne kī . Prov. Mad as he is, his words are full of meaning. ('There is method in his madness'). ṭhikāne chukānā , v. a. To distribute money among the servants on the decease of an aged member of the family. ṭhikānā ḍhūṅḍnā , v. a. To look out for a residence or employment. ṭhikānā k . v. a. 1. To arrange; manage. 2. To make room for. 3. Wom. To find a husband or wife for one. ṭhikānā lagānā , v. a. To find; find out, (one's residence, etc.); trace; detect. ṭhikāne lagānā , v. a. 1. To spend; consume; go through; finish. 2. To put to death; to kill. be-ṭhikāne , adj. 1. Misplaced; out of place; irregular. 2. Incoherent; unconnected; not related. 3. Indeterminate; unsettled; indefinite; vague. 4. Immoderate; disproportioned ( be- had ). 5. Precarious; uncertain. 6. Groundless; unreasonable; conjectural. be-ṭhikāne , adv. Indiscriminately; at random.
